title: Left / Right text align
excerpt: I might be missing this, but there doesn’t seem to be a way to align things like links or email addresses left or right?

posts.author: ChrisCollins (Chris Collins)
posts.cookedHtml: I might be missing this, but there doesn’t seem to be a way to align things like links or email addresses left or right?

posts.author: djb85 (Derek Byrne)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes this would be a nice addition for text formatting

posts.author: Bibi (Bibi)
posts.cookedHtml: It will be great to have this option. My team needs it.

posts.author: tomthack13 (Tom Thackeray)
posts.cookedHtml: Looks like this feature was suggested more than 2 years ago. This is really needed for me to pull in more departments from my company to using Monday.com. Any idea if this is seriously being considered?

posts.author: phil76 (Phil)
posts.cookedHtml: Is this a thing yet? How hard could it be?

posts.author: JimStraatman (Jim)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agree, list of text data is far more readable when left aligned in a table view.

Also want right alignment for dollar amounts, makes seeing relative size of the numeral easier.

posts.author: yoram.golan (Yoram)
posts.cookedHtml:
There is a great need for right to left.
Email notification etc.
in fact al Israeli users get horrible GUI notifications…

posts.author: bobpotter (Bob)
posts.cookedHtml: The ability to align data in text, number, and formula columns would be extremely helpful - especially when there is so much data to look at.

posts.author: marketingpuppet (Marketing)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a feature that should be very simple to add, but extremely impactful.

posts.author: Joe.McCann (Joe)
posts.cookedHtml: So…new to Monday.com and have used a number of CRM products previously. The first thing I noticed, after importing data, was that there is no feature to left or right justify column data. Surely, as previously mentioned in this thread, this capability can not be that difficult to implement – please lets get it done Monday.com dev team!

posts.author: RebH (Rebecca Hogg)
posts.cookedHtml: I don’t understand why all the column are centre-aligned? Or maybe I am missing a trick to left-align them? It makes dashboards very hard to read.

posts.author: JonathanL (Jonathan Livni)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 on supporting text alignment.
Especially in the Notes column…
Left-alignment in LTR languages allows for:

  1. Easier vertical scanning
  2. Less visual noise

posts.author: DGier-IntelliReg (Diana Gier)
posts.cookedHtml: This only has 41 votes, including mine. I’m surprised that it doesn’t have more up-votes because this feature is really needed. There is a reason that Excel left-aligns by default - it’s because, as other posters have mentioned, it’s very difficult to review a lot of data when it’s all middle aligned. How many more votes does this need before Monday will take notice?

posts.author: RebH (Rebecca Hogg)
posts.cookedHtml: Back to check in on this one. Still can’t understand the lack of left-aligning. The center align is so awkward with presenting info on dashboards. Any update on this Monday.com?

posts.author: stephITPM (Stephanie)
posts.cookedHtml:
Screenshot 2023-12-08 1323591157×544 91.3 KB

Screenshot 2023-12-08 132520612×549 45.9 KB

posts.author: sergehufkens (Serge Hufkens)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @monday - time to fix this. This REALLY is a big user experience improvement with minimal effort, not? Left Alignment in Design: The UX/UI Guide for Better Readability | Hriday Checker | Medium

posts.author: Reut.h (Reut Hefetz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there a fix for this issue?
Very basic thing to have

posts.author: accrualbowtie (Ryan Wallace)
posts.cookedHtml: This seems like one of the most basic text formatting options. Any updates or timeline on this?

posts.author: RebH (Rebecca Hogg)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Monday , @CharlotteK - any update on this one?

posts.author: nataliecanestro (Natalie Canestro)
posts.cookedHtml: I would like to vote for this change as well. For text boxes, alignment makes it easier to view data.

posts.author: kathie.austin (Kathie)
posts.cookedHtml: Left justification feature needed please!

posts.author: dbouche1 (Deb Boucher)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree - @monday is this planned?

posts.author: jjattwood (Josh Attwood)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for this @Monday

posts.author: jonmkiwi (Jon Mason)
posts.cookedHtml: @MondayAdmin are you listening to this? This is huge and there is only silence at your end

posts.author: JaimeValmont (Jaime Melton)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes please! We use the description column for a lot of updates at a more detailed glance and having the ability to make it left-aligned would be fantastic.

posts.author: ATFordXIII (Aaron Ford)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, I also need it.
Because the comment bubble is directly tied to task name, and I may not want this visible to my clients, I have to get creative to hide it within documents (in Monday) for which I want to display boards. To do this, I use a formula to pull the task name into a separate column. Unfortunately, this formula then centers the returned value and thus looks bad.

posts.author: hmc (HMC)
posts.cookedHtml: Being restricted like this feels like we’re on dial-up waiting to connect, using floppy disks, with an AOL account, and a black and white printer. It’s 2025, please give us the choice for Left Alignment.

posts.author: Bomberchick (Emma)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this? It’s maddening not being able to left align plain text in a column…

posts.author: Katia (Zilber)
posts.cookedHtml: Super important for right to left languages and emails. The emails sent via the system aren’t readable for Hebrew and Arabic languages.

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Amy here from the monday.com team Thank you so much for your feedback around the need for the left or right alignment feature, we hear you!

At this time, this feature isn’t on our long-term roadmap, as we’re currently focused on other high-impact priorities that are further along in development. That said, we know this comes highly requested, so we’ll continue to evaluate it as we plan ahead.

We’re always listening, and we’ll make sure to share any updates here when anything changes!

posts.author: daniellerch (Daniel Lerch)
posts.cookedHtml:
I know implementation is far more complicated that just adding “text-algin: left;” to a stylesheet. But it’s also a relativley minor feature request that it looks like people have been asking for for over 5 years. This is all folks are asking for:

image584×323 26.3 KB

posts.author: TGuyton (Tim)
posts.cookedHtml: Definitely shocked this has not been implemented. Manager just told me to left align it so that it’s easier to read so it brought me here. Really Monday.com this should be an easy fix unless we are under the wrong assumption. Please reconsider it.

posts.author: JoeNel (Joe)
posts.cookedHtml: upvote - please can we have this feature

posts.author: Cilla (Cilla Namfua)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello, I recently started using Monday.com and it was the first thing that caught my eye. The text non-alignment is not optimum. @Monday.com given the ask since 5 years could you please re-consider. Will be much appreciated.

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello everyone! Thank you for the continued feedback. We are so appreciative of your input—it truly matters. I’m happy to share that this feature is now under active consideration. While we don’t have a timeline to share just yet, we’re making steps towards making this possible! We’ll share a new update with you all this upcoming quarter, so please stay tuned.

posts.author: Sinisan (Raphaël Desberg)
posts.cookedHtml: Incredible. 5 years to add this very small thing. Your roadmap is strange.

posts.author: YSteinberg (Yoel S)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 to this very obvious and minor fix

+100 to monday’s “roadmap” needing heavy fixing. An easy fix with over 130 votes? sheesh
